name: Kaushitaki Upanishad
summary: The Kaushitaki Upanishad describes the path of the soul after death, the nature of prana as Brahman, and the dialogue between Indra and Pratardana. It teaches that prana (life-force) is consciousness and is Brahman.

verses.english_translation: 'Prana is Brahman' — thus indeed said Kaushitaki.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: प्राणः — मुख्यप्राणः — ब्रह्म — चैतन्यस्वरूपः। प्राणस्य ब्रह्मत्वम् उपदिश्यते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Prana — the chief life-force — is Brahman, whose nature is consciousness. This Upanishad teaches the identity of prana with Brahman, the ultimate reality.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वे ह वा अमुं लोकं प्रेत्याभिसंभवन्ति | तान् चन्द्रमाः प्राणेन अनुप्रश्नं पृच्छति ||१.२||
verses.transliteration: sarve ha vā amuṃ lokaṃ pretyābhisaṃbhavanti | tān candramāḥ prāṇena anupraśnaṃ pṛcchati ||1.2||
verses.english_translation: All indeed, after death, go to that world (the moon). The moon questions them through prana.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: मृत्योः परं सर्वे चन्द्रलोकं गच्छन्ति। चन्द्रमाः तान् प्रश्नं पृच्छति — कः असि इति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: After death, all souls go to the lunar world. The moon questions them: 'Who are you?' Those who know the Self pass through; those who do not know return to rebirth. This describes the two paths — the path of the gods (devayana) and the path of the ancestors (pitriyana).

verses.sanskrit: प्रज्ञया वाचं समारुह्य वाचा सर्वाणि नामान्याप्नोति ||३.८||
verses.transliteration: prajñayā vācaṃ samāruhya vācā sarvāṇi nāmāny āpnoti ||3.8||
verses.english_translation: Having mounted speech through consciousness, one attains all names through speech.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: प्रज्ञा — चैतन्यम् — वाचः अधिष्ठाता। प्रज्ञया वाक् प्रवर्तते। एवं सर्वेन्द्रियाणाम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Consciousness is the ground of speech. Speech functions because consciousness activates it. Similarly, all the senses and faculties are activated by consciousness alone. This points to consciousness (prajna) as the ultimate Brahman.

verses.transliteration: prāṇo'smi prajñātmā | taṃ mām āyur amṛtam ity upāssva ||3.2||
verses.english_translation: 'I am prana, whose Self is consciousness. Meditate on me as life and immortality.'
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: प्राणः — मुख्यप्राणः — प्रज्ञात्मा — चैतन्यस्वरूपः। माम् आयुः अमृतम् इति उपास्स्व।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Indra declares to Pratardana: 'I am prana, whose nature is consciousness. Meditate on me as life and immortality.' This identifies prana with Brahman — the conscious life-force that sustains all beings.

word_meanings.meaning: prana alone, whose Self is consciousness
having taken hold of this body

verses.english_translation: Prana alone, whose Self is consciousness, having taken hold of this body, raises it up.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: प्राणः — चैतन्यात्मा — इदम् शरीरम् परिगृह्य — उत्थापयति — जीवयति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Prana, whose nature is consciousness, animates the body. Without prana-consciousness, the body is inert. This is why prana is identified with Brahman — it is the conscious life-force that makes the body alive.

verses.sanskrit: ये वै के चास्माल्लोकात्प्रयन्ति चन्द्रमसमेव ते सर्वे गच्छन्ति ||१.२||
verses.transliteration: ye vai ke cāsmāl lokāt prayanti candramasam eva te sarve gacchanti ||1.2||
verses.english_translation: All who depart from this world go to the moon.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अस्मात् लोकात् प्रयन्ति — मृत्वा — सर्वे चन्द्रलोकम् गच्छन्ति। तत्र चन्द्रः प्रश्नं पृच्छति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: After death, all souls first go to the moon. There the moon questions them. Those who know the Self pass through to higher worlds; those who do not know return to rebirth on earth. This describes the two paths after death.

verses.sanskrit: स य एवं विद्वान् सर्वाणि भूतानि आत्मानमेव पश्यति | सर्वेषु भूतेषु चात्मानम् ||३.१||
verses.transliteration: sa ya evaṃ vidvān sarvāṇi bhūtāni ātmānam eva paśyati | sarveṣu bhūteṣu cātmānam ||3.1||

word_meanings.meaning: sees all beings as the Self

verses.english_translation: One who knows thus sees all beings as the Self alone, and the Self in all beings.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एवम् विद्वान् — प्राणम् ब्रह्म इति जानाति — सः सर्वाणि भूतानि आत्मानम् एव पश्यति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: One who knows prana as Brahman sees all beings as the Self and the Self in all beings. This is the vision of unity — the same teaching as the Isha Upanishad. Knowledge of Brahman leads to seeing the Self everywhere.
